title: CachingStore: lock-never-held-across-await discipline is correct
description: CachingStore acquires the tokio::sync::Mutex, copies the data it needs, drops the lock, then does the network call. This is explicitly documented in the module doc. A contributor may 'simplify' this to hold the lock across the await, which would cause deadlocks on concurrent get() calls (second caller blocks waiting for lock held by first while first awaits network). The current pattern is correct and the comment explains why. Should not be changed.
